channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lagos hosts roundtable to shape future of African music industry
item.description: Lagos is set to host a critical roundtable and music tech conference aimed at shaping the future of Africa's music industry. The event, organized by the Africa Music Business Roundtable (AMBR) and Mus...

item.title: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital completes landmark cardiac procedures for Burundian children
item.description: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital (BMH) and the Jakaya Kikwete Cardiac Institute (JKCI) have successfully performed minimally invasive heart surgery on three Burundian children, marking a significant advancemen...

item.title: Cabinet Secretary Benhur Abalos aids rice distribution to vulnerable sectors
item.description: The rice assistance under the Mula sa Puso para sa Pamilya initiative, funded by the Local Government Support Fund, is being implemented nationwide as part of the President's ongoing support for vulne...

item.title: East Africa Establishes Maritime Safety Hub to Improve Lake Victoria Rescue Operations
item.description: Tanzania has inaugurated East Africa’s first Maritime Rescue Coordination Center on Lake Victoria’s shores in Mwanza, marking a key step in regional efforts to enhance water safety and emergency respo...

item.title: Carnival Announces New Cruise Features and Enhancements
item.description: The article discusses Carnival's recent announcements regarding upcoming cruise innovations, including new onboard amenities, sustainability initiatives, and enhanced passenger experiences. Key points...

item.title: NIA charges six separatist leaders in 1996 Kashmir violence case, three died during proceedings
item.description: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has filed a chargesheet against six separatist leaders of the Hurriyat Conference in connection with a 1996 case of mob violence and indiscriminate firing on po...

item.title: Suspect in UK Family Murder Arrested in South Africa
item.description: Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, a British citizen of Zimbabwean heritage, has been arrested by South African police in Johannesburg following allegations of murdering his wife and two daughters in the UK. T...

item.title: Senate Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte: Legal Arguments on Assassination Threat and Impeachability
item.description: The Senate impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte entered its first week, focusing on the legal debate over whether her alleged assassination threats against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. c...

item.title: Former MP Ann Widdecombe found dead with head wound, murder suspect arrested
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative MP and Strictly Come Dancing star, was found dead at her Devon home with severe head injuries and blood covering her body. Police arrested a 26-year-old white Bri...

item.title: Witness Describes Eviction and Imprisonment of Tanzanian Artist Baba Levo
item.description: A witness in the legal case against Tanzanian artist Baba Levo has testified about his alleged eviction from his residence and subsequent imprisonment, providing critical details on the procedures fol...

item.title: Spain and Belgium Draw 1-1 in World Cup Quarter-Final
item.description: In a thrilling encounter at SoFi Stadium, Spain and Belgium contested a tightly fought World Cup quarter-final that ended in a 1-1 draw. Spain took the lead through Fabian Ruiz's close-range finish af...

item.title: UK Nuclear Waste Management and Global Environmental Concerns
item.description: The article discusses the UK government's plan to address nuclear waste management, highlighting challenges in safe storage and disposal. It emphasizes the need for international cooperation to ensure...

item.title: South African fugitive arrested in Johannesburg over UK family murders
item.description: Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, a fugitive wanted by British authorities for the murders of his wife, Nothabo Zandile Tshuma, and their two daughters, Natalie and Nala, was arrested in Johannesburg by South...

item.title: Vendr.ng Streamlines Estate Management with Digital Solutions
item.description: Vendr.ng, a Nigerian property tech platform, is solving key challenges faced by estate managers in Nigeria's growing real estate sector. The platform provides digital tools for utility management, res...

item.title: The Risks of Holding Excess Cash in Investment Portfolios
item.description: The article discusses the potential drawbacks of holding excessive cash in investment portfolios. While cash can serve practical purposes like emergency funds or short-term needs, maintaining too much...

item.title: House GOP Rep. Blocks Voting Rules to Push Controversial Voting Restrictions Bill

item.title: Athletes Zakithi Nene and Jo-Ane du Plessis Compete in Diamond League Events
item.description: Zakithi Nene, a South African sprinter, delivered a strong performance in the 400m at the Diamond League meet in Monaco, finishing fifth with a time of 44.24 seconds. This marks his second-fastest tim...

item.title: Senator Marcoleta's Family Seeks Visits from VP and Senator Amid Health Concerns
item.description: Sen. Rodante Marcoleta and his family have formally requested Vice President Sara Duterte and Sen. Robin Padilla to be allowed visitation rights in his hospitalization, according to a statement by PNP...

item.title: Padel's rise in luxury travel: A new trend among high-end travellers
item.description: Luxury hotels across Marbella to the Maldives are increasingly installing padel courts, catering to high-end travellers seeking unique experiences. Padel, a fast-growing racquet sport blending tennis ...

item.title: FCI Tightens Oversight on Subsidized Rice Use by Distilleries
item.description: The Food Corporation of India (FCI) has announced stricter measures to ensure the proper use of subsidized rice by distilleries involved in ethanol production. This comes amid concerns about misuse of...
